summary: Path-sliced VI matching cleanup from a larger split-up branch, touching only src/melee/vi/vi0502.c. The change refactors un_8031E444_OnEnter locals to more specific names and types, treats the entry argument as a ViCharaDesc instead of raw u8 input, and applies several small expression/formatting changes that likely supported matching without broad subsystem changes.

key_files.role: Only changed file; updates un_8031E444_OnEnter in the VI 0502 visual scene setup path.

what_changed: Changed un_8031E444_OnEnter argument handling from a raw u8* input to a ViCharaDesc* desc.
Replaced char_kind/input indexing with named descriptor fields: p1_char_index, p1_costume_index, and p2_costume_index.
Renamed a reused generic HSD_GObj* gobj local into role-specific locals: fog_gobj, light_gobj, camera_gobj, and model_gobj.
Changed the character animation archive lookup to use viGetCharAnimByIndex(char_index), where char_index is desc->p1_char_index.
Removed the GXColor pointer cast assignment to erase_colors_vi0502 and assigned fog->color directly.
Introduced an extra HSD_JObj* local named new_var and routed some model object setup and animation calls through it.
Adjusted small matching-sensitive expressions and literals, including 0.0f to 0.0F and gm_8016895C's third argument from 0 to (un_804D6F90->models[i] != NULL) * 0.
PR body states this was split from #2617 by directory/subsystem to make matching improvements easier to review, with scope limited to src/melee/vi.
smart_moves: Kept the slice narrowly scoped to src/melee/vi/vi0502.c, reducing review risk for a larger matching-improvement branch.
Recovered the semantic type of the scene-entry argument as ViCharaDesc, replacing magic byte offsets with named fields.
Used role-specific GObj local names to document scene construction order: fog, light, camera, then models.
Preserved behavior while making decompilation intent clearer around character archive loading and final vi0502_8031E124 setup arguments.
Used local variable and expression shaping rather than broad logic changes, a common safer tactic for matching-oriented PRs.
decomp_lessons: When an entry argument is actually a known descriptor struct, prefer casting once to that descriptor type and using named fields instead of raw byte indexing.
Splitting a larger matching branch by directory/subsystem can make small decomp improvements easier to review and merge.
Generic reused locals can be renamed to object-role locals without changing behavior, improving readability and making future matching work less error-prone.
Matching-sensitive code may require apparently redundant aliases or expressions, such as assigning jobj to another local or multiplying a known non-null check by zero.
Direct struct assignment can replace casted pointer assignment when the destination type is already known, as with erase_colors_vi0502 = fog->color.
naming_conventions: Use *_gobj suffixes for HSD_GObj* locals whose role is known, e.g. fog_gobj, light_gobj, camera_gobj, model_gobj.
Use char_index for a character-animation index when the code calls viGetCharAnimByIndex, rather than a broader CharacterKind name if evidence points to an index field.
Use descriptor field names from ViCharaDesc such as p1_char_index, p1_costume_index, and p2_costume_index instead of input[0], input[1], and input[3].
The introduced name new_var is not semantically strong; evidence suggests it was likely retained for matching/local allocation rather than as a final naming ideal.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Recovered a struct pointer cast for arg: desc = (ViCharaDesc*) arg.
Replaced raw array accesses with struct-field accesses while preserving the same data flow into viGetCharAnimByIndex and vi0502_8031E124.
Split one reused gobj local into several typed role locals, likely influencing register allocation/lifetimes while clarifying object ownership.
Introduced a jobj alias new_var before HSD_GObjObject_80390A70 and HSD_JObjReqAnimAll, likely to shape local lifetimes or register use.
Used the no-op expression (un_804D6F90->models[i] != NULL) * 0 instead of literal 0 in gm_8016895C, suggesting a matching-motivated expression form.
Changed floating constants from 0.0f to 0.0F; this is stylistically equivalent in C but appears in the matching diff.

review_feedback.feedback: The PR was explicitly split from #2617 by directory/subsystem so matching improvements were easier to review.
No inline review comments or formal review feedback were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or eliminate the temporary new_var local in src/melee/vi/vi0502.c.
Search for other OnEnter functions that still treat descriptor arguments as raw u8* arrays.
Compare PR #2617 for the full unsplit matching branch context and any related VI changes.
Check whether ViCharaDesc is documented in headers and whether all p1/p2 costume fields are consistently named across VI scenes.
